mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 55% of instances when UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production levels reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases, with a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate wage income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the coefficient in a linear regression model.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses a 2.5 percent annual threshold, anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression force negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields a negative information coefficient for 20-day returns across all tested subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in over 55% of cases.
